_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning about the continuities and changes in the nature of warfare across the world wars — including technology, tactics, and the impact on civilians.

_source.w: We are learning about the continuities and changes in the nature of warfare across the world wars — including technology, tactics, and the impact on civilians.
y: Warfare transformed dramatically between 1914 and 1945 — understanding this helps us grasp the escalating human cost.
Changes in warfare technology and tactics have implications that extend into the present day.
The increasing impact of war on civilians is one of the most significant changes of the 20th century.
eg: Comparing the trench warfare of WWI with the mobile, mechanised warfare of WWII.
Investigating how technology — aircraft, submarines, radar, code-breaking, nuclear weapons — transformed warfare.
Analysing the increasing impact on civilian populations — from aerial bombing to the Holocaust.
Discussing how chemical and biological weapons raised new ethical questions about the conduct of war.
Evaluating how propaganda and information warfare evolved between the two conflicts.
Examining how the nature of warfare has continued to change since 1945.
